title: Edge network for building services
abstract: A tintable window is described having a tintable coating, e.g., an electrochromic device coating, for regulating light transmitted through the window. In some embodiments, the window has a transparent display in the window's viewable region. Transparent displays may be substantially transparent when not in use, or when the window is viewed in a direction facing away from the transparent display. Windows may have sensors for receiving user commands and/or for monitoring environmental conditions. Transparent displays can display graphical user interfaces to, e.g., control window functions. Windows, as described herein, offer an alternative display to conventional projectors, TVs, and monitors. Windows may also be configured to receive, transmit, or block wireless communications from passing through the window. A window control system may share computational resources between controllers (e.g., at different windows). In some cases, the computational resources of the window control system are utilized by other building systems and devices.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS what is claimed is: 1. A method of conducting edge computing in a building, the method comprising: receiving software and/or data, via a connection to an external network, from a remote site that is remote from the building, wherein the software and/or data is a copy or instance of a master version of software and/or data stored on the remote site; installing or storing the software and/or data on a first data storage device on a building data communications network, wherein the building data communications network comprises a plurality of processors disposed within the building and a plurality of data storage devices, including the first data storage device, disposed within the building, wherein the building data communications network further comprises communication lines connecting the plurality of processors and the plurality of data storage devices, and wherein the communication lines are disposed in or on outer walls and/or one or more fa ades of the building; and providing the software and/or data from the first storage device, or providing results of executing the software, to a computational device in the building via the building data communications network. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the external network is a public network and wherein the building data communications network is a private network. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the external network is the internet, and wherein the building data communications network comprises a connection to the internet. 4. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the computational device in the building is a handheld computational device, a laptop, a terminal, or a desk top computer. 5. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the computational device in the building is a processor configured to provide or assist in providing a building service. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the building service is selected from the group 

 consisting of a HVAC service, a security service, a building lighting service, an occupant information delivery service, and an electrically tintable window control service. 7. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of window controllers comprising electrical circuits configured to control tint states of an electrically tintable window installed in the building. 8. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a display device disposed on a window in the building. 9. The method of any of claims 1-6, wherein no processors of the plurality of processors are provided in electrically tintable window controllers. 10. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein no processors of the plurality of processors are dedicated to controlling electrically switchable window tint states. 11. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of antennas and a plurality of radios or transceivers electrically connected to the plurality of antennas and wherein the plurality of radios or transceivers is configured to send and/or receive wireless communications via the plurality of antennas. 12. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of sensors comprising a temperature sensor, an irradiance sensor, a humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, a motion sensor, an occupant tracking sensor, a biometric sensor, and/or a VOC sensor. 13. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the building data communications network comprises a vertical data plane that links computational nodes on different floors of the building. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the vertical data plane comprises a network switch and communications links configured to transmit data at speeds of at least about 1 gigabit/second. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the communications links comprise current carrying lines, optical fibers, and/or wireless connections. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the vertical data plane comprises a first control panel on a first floor of the building and a second control panel on a second floor of the building, and wherein the first and second control panels are linked on building data communications network in a manner that supports gigabit/sec Ethernet communications. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of trunk lines connected to the first control panel, extending to locations on the first floor of the building, and arranged in manner that provides network service to a plurality of network nodes on the first floor. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of drop lines providing data connections between the trunk lines and the plurality of network nodes on the first floor. 19. The method of any of the foregoing claims, further comprising: installing the plurality of processors, the plurality of data storage devices, and the communication lines during construction of the building. 20. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the communication lines are disposed in one or more mullions of the building. 21. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the first data storage device is located in master controller or a control panel connected to the building data communications network. 22. The method of any of the foregoing claims, further comprising executing the software on an edge processor of the building data communications network and providing the results of executing the software on the edge processor, to the computational device. 23. The method of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the software comprises video conferencing software. 24. The method of any of claims 1-21, wherein the data comprises a subset of data in a database stored on the remote site. 25. The method of any of claims 1-21, wherein the data comprises a patch or an upgrade to software installed on the computational device in the building. 26. The method of any of the foregoing claims, further comprising receiving an update to the software and/or data, via the connection to an external network, from the remote site; and installing the update applying the update to the software and/or data on a first data storage device on a building data communications network. 27. A building data communications system comprising: a building data communications network comprising: (a) a plurality of processors disposed within the building; (b) a plurality of data storage devices disposed within the building; (c) communication lines connecting the plurality of processors and the plurality of data storage devices, wherein the communication lines are disposed in or on outer walls and/or one or more fa ades of the building; (d) a connection to an external network on the building data communications network; and (e) an edge computing processing device or system comprising program instructions for: (i) receiving software and/or data, via the connection to the external network, from a remote site that is remote from the building; (ii) installing or store the software and/or data on a first data storage device 

 that is one of the plurality of data storage devices disposed on the building data communications network; and (iii) providing the software and/or data from the first storage device, or providing results of executing the software, to a computational device in the building via the building data communications network. 28. The building data communications system of claim 27, further comprising power lines in the building frame, wherein the power lines are configured to provide power to the plurality of processors. 29. The building data communications system of claim 27 or 28, wherein the external network is a public network and the wherein the building data communications network is a private network. 30. The building data communications system of any of claims 27-29, wherein the external network is the internet, and wherein the building data communications network comprises a connection to the internet. 31. The building data communications system of any of claims 27-30, wherein the computational device in the building is a handheld computational device, a laptop, a terminal, or a desk top computer. 32. The building data communications system of any of claims 27-30, wherein the computational device in the building is a processor configured to provide or assist in providing a building service selected from the group consisting of a HVAC service, a security service, a building lighting service, an occupant information delivery service, and an electrically tintable window control service. 33. The building data communications system of any of claims 27-32, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of window controllers comprising electrical circuits configured to control tint states of an electrically tintable window installed in the building. 34. The building data communications system any of claims 27-32, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a display device disposed on a window in the building. 35. The building data communications system any of claims 27-32, wherein no processors of the plurality of processors are provided in electrically tintable window controllers. 36. The building data communications system any of claims 27-32, wherein no processors of the plurality of processors are dedicated to controlling electrically switchable window tint states. 37. The building data communications system any of claims 27-36, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of antennas and a plurality of radios or transceivers electrically connected to the plurality of antennas and wherein the plurality of radios or transceivers is configured to send and/or receive wireless communications via the plurality of antennas. 38. The building data communications system any of claims 27-37, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of sensors comprising a temperature sensor, an irradiance sensor, a humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, a motion sensor, an occupant tracking sensor, a biometric sensor, and/or a VOC sensor. 39. The building data communications system any of claims 27-38, wherein the building data communications network comprises a vertical data plane that links computational nodes on different floors of the building. 40. The building data communications system of claim 39, wherein the vertical data plane comprises a network switch and communications links configured to transmit data at speeds of at least about 1 gigabit/second. 41. The building data communications system of claim 40, wherein the communications links comprise current carrying lines, optical fibers, and/or wireless connections. 42. The building data communications system of claim 39, wherein the vertical data plane comprises a first control panel on a first floor of the building and a second control panel on a second floor of the building, and wherein the first and second control panels are linked on building data communications network in a manner that supports gigabit/sec Ethernet communications. 43. The building data communications system of claim 42, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of trunk lines connected to the first control panel, extending to locations on the first floor of the building, and arranged in manner that provides network service to a plurality of network nodes on the first floor. 44. The building data communications system of claim 43, wherein the building data communications network further comprises a plurality of drop lines providing data connections between the trunk lines and the plurality of network nodes on the first floor. 45. The building data communications system any of claims 27-44, wherein the plurality of processors, the plurality of data storage devices, and the communication lines were installed during construction of the building. 46. The building data communications system any of claims 27-45, wherein the communication lines are disposed in one or more mullions of the building. 47. The building data communications system any of claims 27-46, wherein the first data storage device is located in master controller or a control panel connected to the building data communications network. 48. The building data communications system any of claims 27-47, wherein the edge computing processing device or system further comprises program instructions for 

 executing the software and providing the results of executing the software to the computational device. 49. The building data communications system any of claims 27-48, wherein the software comprises video conferencing software. 50. The building data communications system any of claims 27-47, wherein the data comprises a subset of data in a database stored on the remote site. 51. The building data communications system any of claims 27-47, wherein the data comprises a patch or an upgrade to software installed on the computational device in the building. 52. The building data communications system any of claims 27-51, wherein the edge computing processing device or system further comprises program instructions for: receiving an update to the software and/or data, via the connection to an external network, from the remote site; and installing the update applying the update to the software and/or data on a first data storage device on a building data communications network.
